text.title: Three Audience Cuts from the Canonical Base
text.summary: Investor cut, GTM cut, and pricing-tier cut: how each derives from the canonical market-research.md base.

rule.statement: All three cuts must derive from the same canonical base at .aidocs/product_docs/market-research.md. If the investor cut and GTM cut disagree on the SAM number, something is wrong.
rule.rationale: Divergent numbers across deck, GTM plan, and pricing model undermine credibility with partners, GTM team, and product managers simultaneously. One canonical source of truth prevents drift.

list.item: One slide, three numbers (TAM / SAM / SOM), three sources, one chart, 40 words maximum
TAM labeled explicitly as "if-100% scenario" in a footnote — partners reward honesty
SOM growth arrow must match the Y5 ARR in the financial model exactly
Include 2 public-comp equivalents at the same stage for instant credibility
Round aggressively for the slide ($8B), preserve precision in the appendix ($8.27B) — never reverse

rule.statement: The investor cut must fit on one slide. If it does not, the agent has not finished the work.
rule.rationale: An overloaded market slide signals that the founder cannot explain the opportunity succinctly. Partners use this as a signal about pitch clarity.

list.item: Split SAM into mutually exclusive acquisition channels: PLG (low CAC, low ACV), outbound (high CAC, high ACV), partner (medium), content/SEO (decaying low CAC)
Each bucket has a SAM ceiling — total reachable accounts via that channel only
CAC envelope per bucket comes from pricing-research.md; any tier where bucket-CAC exceeds 0.33 × tier-LTV is GTM-blocked
Geography splits must align with the pricing cut — no GTM motion in regions without localized pricing

rule.statement: GTM channel buckets must be mutually exclusive. Double-counting a customer between PLG and outbound is the most common GTM-cut audit failure.
rule.rationale: Overlapping channel buckets inflate the reachable market and produce GTM plans that exceed the actual addressable population.

section.title: Pricing-Tier Cut

list.item: For each pricing tier: tier-SAM = SAM × tier-mix percentage, tier-SOM = tier-SAM × realistic-share-for-tier
Tier-mix reality: most SaaS land 60-70% Starter, 25-30% Pro, 5-10% Enterprise — a deck claiming 40% Enterprise requires evidence
LTV uses tier-specific churn, not blended rate — Starter churn is often 3-5x Enterprise churn
Any tier whose Y3 SOM is below 6 months of one engineer's salary should be cut from the deck — distraction
Express tier-SOM in customer count first ("12,000 Pro seats"), dollars second — investors trust counts more

section.reference: Script decomposing SAM/SOM per tier with LTV/CAC sanity check
